title: stellaria graminea var. pilosula
intro: Stellaria graminea var. pilosula, also known as creeping starwort or hairy chickweed, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and minor burns. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, creeping starwort is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its medicinal properties.
